schedule.title: Reed's Academic Plan
schedule.subtitle: Fall 2026 Modification: Management & Innovation vs. Information Systems

currentPath.rationale: Reed continues in Management & Innovation with MKT365 remaining as an elective. All four Fall 2026 courses are preserved as required or elective credits toward this concentration. BUS225 is scheduled for Spring 2027 as the missing required course.

fall2026Swap.addRationale: CIS240 is the foundational prerequisite for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360 — three required IS junior/senior courses Reed currently cannot access. Adding it in Fall 2026 immediately unblocks the IS critical path and is the highest-priority course with no unmet prerequisites.

insights.text: Switching to Information Systems and adding CIS240 in Fall 2026 immediately unlocks three currently blocked required courses (CIS320, CIS355, CIS360), making Spring 2028 graduation feasible on the minimal-load path.
CIS411 (IT for Business) satisfies both an IS elective requirement and the IT-for-Business certificate, efficiently serving dual purposes within the 12-credit ceiling.

Under the Management & Innovation path, MKT365 is retained as an elective but contributes less urgency than CIS240 would for IS; Reed's existing MGT coursework (MGT301, MGT310, MGT340, MGT320 in progress) already provides a strong foundation.
Both paths preserve the 12-credit-per-semester ceiling and keep BUS225 in Spring 2027 as required, maintaining Reed's minimal-load preference throughout.
CIS370 (in progress Fall 2026) counts as a required IS junior course regardless of which path Reed chooses, meaning no credits are wasted if Reed switches to Information Systems.

fall2026Swap.addName: Introduction to Information Systems and Programming
fall2026Swap.addRationale: CIS240 is the foundational prerequisite blocking CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360 — three required IS courses Reed cannot access without it. Taking CIS240 in Fall 2026 immediately unlocks the core IS upper-division sequence and is the single highest-impact swap available. MKT365 is not required for Information Systems and is safely dropped.

courses.name: Introduction to Information Systems and Programming

schedule.note: Study abroad semester; 5-course 15cr cap applies. BUS225 moved to Fall 2027.
BUS479 final semester only, paired with 3 other courses to reach 12cr. CIS411 satisfies IT-for-Business certificate elective requirement.

insights.text: Swapping MKT365 for CIS240 in Fall 2026 is the critical unlock for an IS switch — without CIS240, three required IS courses (CIS320, CIS355, CIS360) remain inaccessible and Spring 2028 graduation becomes very difficult to achieve.
Both paths respect the 12cr hard ceiling every non-abroad semester and the 15cr abroad cap in Spring 2027, keeping Reed's load minimal throughout.
Under the IS switch, Fall 2027 is packed at 12cr with four required courses (BUS225, MGT410, CIS320, CIS355), so Reed should be prepared for a heavier return semester after study abroad.

The Management & Innovation path carries less scheduling risk — BUS225 is the only missing required course — but leaves IS prerequisites permanently unresolved, making a future switch increasingly costly.
CIS411 (IT for Business) in the IS Spring 2028 schedule satisfies both a required elective slot and the IT-for-Business certificate, giving Reed a credential advantage with no extra coursework.

MGT301 was completed with a C+, which meets minimum passing requirements; Reed should confirm with the registrar whether the IS concentration accepts MGT301 at that grade, as some programs require a C or better in all required courses.

fall2026Swap.addRationale: CIS240 is the foundational prerequisite gate for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360 — all required IS junior/senior courses Reed currently cannot take. Swapping MKT365 for CIS240 in Fall 2026 unlocks the entire IS course sequence and is the single highest-leverage change Reed can make. MKT365 is not required for the IS primary concentration and can be dropped without consequence to IS completion.

schedule.note: Abroad semester — same IB structure as current path; Group 3 auto-satisfied; 15cr abroad cap applied
CIS240 completion in Fall 2026 unlocks CIS320 and CIS355 here; BUS225 moved per abroad rule; CIS411 satisfies IT-for-Business certificate elective
Final semester — BUS479 capstone anchors; CIS360 completes senior IS requirements; two electives reach 12cr ceiling

insights.text: Swapping MKT365 for CIS240 in Fall 2026 is the critical unlock for the IS path — without it, CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360 are all blocked, making a Spring 2028 IS graduation impossible.
Both paths preserve the Spring 2027 abroad semester intact at 15 credits with the three IB required courses plus two Group 2 selections, and both auto-satisfy Group 3.
Neither path exceeds the 12cr hard ceiling in any domestic semester, and the 15cr abroad cap is respected in Spring 2027.
BUS479 is correctly reserved as the final-semester capstone in Spring 2028 in both paths, paired with three supporting courses to reach the 12cr ceiling.
The IS switch path earns the IT-for-Business certificate by including CIS411 in Fall 2027, adding a credential with no additional course load beyond what IS elective requirements already demand.
Reed's completed MGT301 (C+) meets the senior IS requirement — no retake needed — and MKT300 (B) similarly satisfies the senior IS marketing requirement, so neither path requires extra remediation.
If Reed stays in Management & Innovation, MKT365 completed in Fall 2026 still counts toward the IB Group 1 requirement, preserving full value of that course even without the IS switch.

insights.text: CIS240 (Systems Analysis and Design) is the single most critical course in Reed's remaining plan — it is a prerequisite for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360, and is currently missing. It must be taken in Spring 2027 to keep the remaining IS curriculum on track for Spring 2028 graduation.
BUS225 is a missing required course and has been scheduled for Spring 2027 per the non-abroad routing rule.

CIS411 (IT for Business) is scheduled in Spring 2027 and satisfies the IT-for-Business certificate elective, supporting Reed's minimal-load path while earning credential value.

schedule.title: Reed's Academic Plan
schedule.subtitle: B.S. Business Administration — Information Systems Concentration

semesters.abroadNote: Study abroad semester — up to 15cr via international coursework. BUS225 deferred to Fall 2027 per abroad rule.

blockers.issue: CIS240 is missing and is a prerequisite for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360. All three are blocked until CIS240 is completed. Earliest opportunity is Fall 2027 after returning from abroad.

insights.text: CIS240 was never completed and is a hard prerequisite for three senior IS requirements (CIS320, CIS355, CIS360). This creates a critical bottleneck — all three must be taken after CIS240 is done in Fall 2027, which means they all land in the final Spring 2028 semester alongside BUS479. This is tight but feasible at 12cr.
BUS225 is deferred to Fall 2027 per the study-abroad rule. Reed should confirm this is acceptable with their advisor, as BUS225 is a missing required course.
CIS411 (IT for Business) is scheduled in Fall 2027 and satisfies both an IS elective and the IT-for-Business certificate requirement simultaneously, maximizing efficiency under the minimal-load path.
The plan stays at or under 12 credits every domestic semester, honoring the minimal-load ceiling throughout.
BUS479 is correctly placed in the final semester (Spring 2028) alongside exactly 3 other courses to reach the 12cr cap.

MGT410 was listed as a Fall 2027 junior required course in the rules, but does not appear in the provided IS concentration requirements. Reed should confirm with their advisor whether MGT410 is required — if so, it must be added to Fall 2027, which would push the credit count to 15cr and require dropping one course (recommend dropping CIS310 elective, which can move to Spring 2028 if needed, or be dropped entirely if 4 electives are already satisfied by abroad credits).

Only 2 of 4 required IS electives (CIS411, CIS310) are explicitly scheduled in domestic semesters. Reed should verify whether any study-abroad courses satisfy IS elective requirements. If not, CIS340 or another list course should be added in Fall 2027, which would require dropping CIS310 or adjusting load — consult advisor before finalizing.

Originally flagged for Fall 2027 as junior required — confirm with advisor whether this is required for IS concentration or if CIS240 takes priority. Could replace BUS225 or CIS411 in Fall 2027 if required, keeping under 12cr.

insights.text: CIS240 is the critical bottleneck — it is missing and is a prerequisite for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360. Reed must register for CIS240 in Fall 2027 immediately upon return from abroad, or the Spring 2028 final semester becomes infeasible.

MGT410 was specified as a junior required course for Fall 2027 but is not included because the Fall 2027 semester is already at 12cr with BUS225, CIS240, CIS411, and CIS310. Reed should verify with an advisor whether MGT410 is mandatory for the IS concentration — if so, one elective may need to shift.

Study abroad Spring 2027 satisfies the IB Group 3 requirement automatically and allows all three IB required courses (FIN475, MGT435, MGT475) plus both Group 2 courses to be completed in one semester under the 15cr abroad cap.
CIS411 (IT for Business) is scheduled in Fall 2027 and satisfies the IT-for-Business certificate elective requirement, efficiently doubling as both an IS elective and a cert-satisfying course.
BUS479 capstone is correctly reserved for the final semester (Spring 2028) alongside three other courses, bringing the semester to exactly 12cr as required.
BUS225 is moved to Fall 2027 per the abroad rule, keeping Spring 2027 fully dedicated to the IB abroad curriculum without exceeding the 15cr abroad cap.

Reed has completed MGT301 with a C+. Some programs require a minimum grade of C in required courses — Reed should verify with the registrar that C+ meets the threshold for the IS senior required course.

The fourth IS elective slot is not yet filled in this plan. Reed should choose one more course from: CIS350, CIS410, CIS413, CIS455, CIS563, CIS575, CIS576, or CIS355 (once CIS240 is complete). This could be added to Fall 2027 only if MGT410 is confirmed not required — otherwise the semester is at ceiling.

notes: CIS240 must be completed Fall 2027 to unlock CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360 in the final semester.
Spring 2028 carries only 12 credits to accommodate BUS479 as a final-semester capstone with 3 companion courses.

Two IS elective slots (CIS340 or CIS350 recommended) remain unfilled due to the 15cr cap. Reed may petition to add one in Fall 2027 if BUS225 or MGT410 can be deferred, or accept graduation with the minimum 4 electives if CIS411 and CIS310 count toward that total per department approval.
IB Group 3 is auto-satisfied by the Spring 2027 abroad semester.
IB Group 2 is satisfied by ECON440 and POLS232 taken abroad (6 credits total).
MGT301 grade of C+ should be reviewed with advisor to confirm it meets the minimum grade requirement for the major.

insights.text: CIS240 is missing and is a prerequisite for CIS320, CIS355, and CIS360. It has been placed in Fall 2027 immediately after study abroad — Reed cannot take those three courses until CIS240 is complete, which compresses all three into the final semester. Reed should explore whether CIS240 can be taken online or at a host institution during the abroad term to relieve final-semester pressure.

Study abroad Spring 2027 fully satisfies IB Group 3 and simultaneously completes the three required IB courses (FIN475, MGT435, MGT475) plus both Group 2 courses, making the International Business secondary concentration achievable without any additional domestic semesters.

CIS411 (IT for Business) satisfies the IT-for-Business certificate elective requirement and counts toward the four IS elective slots, efficiently serving both the concentration and certificate in a single course.
BUS225 is missing from the transcript and has been moved to Fall 2027 per the abroad rule. Reed should confirm with the registrar that no other core requirements depend on BUS225 as a prerequisite.

The Spring 2028 capstone semester carries only 12 credits (4 courses including BUS479), which is below the 15cr ceiling and meets the BUS479 rule requiring exactly 3 companion courses for a 12cr total.

Reed currently has only 2 confirmed IS electives planned (CIS411, CIS310). The concentration requires 4. With the 15cr cap and CIS240 also needed in Fall 2027, there is no room to add more electives without exceeding the cap. Reed should meet with their advisor to determine whether CIS340 or CIS350 can substitute for another course or whether a fifth semester or overload petition is needed.
